definition: The copper/gold ratio has broken down as an FX-regime indicator since 2022: correlation with US 10Y yields collapsed from r~0.7 to r~0.2. Copper is constrained by Chinese construction (-25% from peak) and subdued PMIs; gold is propelled by central bank buying and geopolitical hedging, decoupled from real-rate anchor. The two metals are now driven by orthogonal factors.
evidence: Debate pair_1: 'the most immediately actionable claim in either analysis'
Correlation collapse is a verifiable statistical fact
Copper drivers: Chinese construction decline, subdued PMIs
Gold drivers: central bank buying (1,000+ tonnes annually), geopolitical hedging
open_questions: Whether the ratio will re-correlate if Chinese construction stabilizes
What alternative ratio or indicator should replace copper/gold for FX regime identification
